page: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. Managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with an exceptionally high memory capacity of 168.0 GB, yielding 42.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 472 GB disk, and the server is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server excels in single-threaded operations, achieving top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Conversely, multi-core and parallel performance is more limited, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression falling into the weak category. Large-block read-only memory bandwidth is also weak at 36.17 GB/sec, and LLM inference tasks for larger models show poor performance.

From a resource-density perspective, the FX8-4mds_v2 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. By bundling 168.0 GB of RAM with only 4 vCPUs, it allows organizations to run memory-intensive applications without paying for unnecessary CPU cores. This makes it highly suitable for in-memory databases, caching systems, and single-threaded text or data processing pipelines. However, it is poorly suited for highly parallelized compute tasks, multi-threaded compiling, or large-scale AI inference.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers an exceptional memory capacity of 168.0 GB alongside 472 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks highlight outstanding single-core performance, with top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and text processing, as well as strong single-threaded compression. However, multi-core scaling and large-block memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive, low-vCPU workloads. It is highly recommended for in-memory databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing, while being less suitable for highly parallelized compute or large-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 vCPUs, 168.0 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core performance for in-memory databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 168.0 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring exceptional single-core performance and local storage for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
Massive 168.0 GB RAM capacity providing 42.0 GB per core
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark results
Qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-vCPU applications
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and single-threaded processing
